mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135613.0 thousand as of March 2012 indicates sustained employment growth
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with positive labor momentum
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% for three consecutive months, the zero-lower-bound monetary accommodation regime sustains equity valuations by suppressing real discount rates and encouraging risk-asset allocation. This threshold-triggered signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of March 2012 indicates post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% for 3+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signals synchronized consumer and residential demand expansion, which jointly amplifies corporate profit expectations and equity risk appetite. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 366166.0 million and HOUST at 1665.0 thousand as of February 2012
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining more than 1.5% over three months signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side contraction, which compresses corporate margins and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 1.5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.7063 index points as of February 2012
failure_condition: 3-month INDPRO declines > 1.5% fail to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of future monetary tightening, which increases equity discount rates and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate higher borrowing costs and reduced monetary accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.7 index points as of February 2012
regime_hint: inflation acceleration regime with tightening expectations
failure_condition: CPI year-over-year growth > 2.5% fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens discretionary spending and corporate earnings. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when unemployment enters the elevated regime.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of March 2012 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: elevated unemployment regime with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.0% fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) signals robust aggregate demand and capital formation, which sustains equity valuations through higher expected corporate cash flows. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% and HOUST increases quarter-over-quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11388.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2011
regime_hint: above-trend GDP growth with residential investment acceleration
failure_condition: Joint GDP growth > 2.5% and HOUST increase fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growing above 0.5% month-over-month sustained over three months signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profitability and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprice earnings upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO grows more than 0.5% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.7063 index points as of February 2012 indicates post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: 3-month sustained INDPRO growth > 0.5% monthly fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
